title: selliera exigua
intro: Selliera exigua, also known as the dwarf selliera or New Zealand bogwort, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Selliera exigua include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture, and it may also be used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
